title: Physiologically Based PK (PBPK)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Physiologically Based PK (PBPK): PBPK model: mechanistic organ-level pharmacokinetics connecting tissue concentrations to plasma via physiological parameters. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.pbpk_organs…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; in_vitro_in_vivo_extrapolation_IVIVE_bias, enzyme_induction_inhibition set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dA_org/dt = Q_org*(C_arterial - C_venous_org) - CL_int*C_org; conservation of mass across organs
E.world_state_x: tissue-specific partition coefficients Kp and metabolic clearance CL_int per organ
E.observation_y: plasma and (optionally) tissue concentration-time profiles from in vivo or in vitro data

W.regime: Existence of the recovered PBPK_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); in_vitro_in_vivo_extrapolation_IVIVE_bias dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: plasma concentration within 2-fold; tissue Kp within 3-fold. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |in_vitro_in_vivo_extrapolation_IVIVE_bias|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_organs * N_timesteps) per ODE integration per iteration
